title: update-block-item cannot delete a field — a null update sets the key to null instead of removing it
description: update-block-item --updates '{"field":null}' sets the field to null on the item rather than removing the key; no op surface deletes a field from an item. The observed workaround is upsert-block-item full-shape replacement. Hit removing a stray status field from the de-ephemeralize-at-source conventions rule: the null landed on disk ({"status":null}) and only an upsert replace dropped the key. Uninvestigated — root cause, shape, class (is field-deletion missing across the whole update/merge surface?), and prior-art are for the investigating agent.

evidence.reference: the triggering instance: update-block-item --match '{"id":"de-ephemeralize-at-source"}' --updates '{"status":null}' produced status:null on the item; upsert-block-item full-replace removed it

impact: A filed item cannot be cleaned of a stray field through the merge-update surface; the only route is a full-shape replacement, which re-states the whole item to remove one key.
proposed_resolution: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
